judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 covers all five expectations including the message-ordering rule, while Report 2 hits four but misses the lead-with-impact/remediation restructuring point, making Report 1 the stronger answer.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` shows checkout failures began at 14:03 but the first page didn't fire until 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap, not "within minutes."

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` shows checkout failures began at 14:03 and the first page-triggering alert did not fire until 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap, not "minutes."

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states: "During the window 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing; 37 were unrecoverable and refunded."

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and had to be refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` states the contractual monthly uptime SLA is 99.9%, and any month below that is a breach that "owes service credits per the customer agreement." `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 (the incident month) at 99.77%, below the 99.9% threshold.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md` defines the contractual monthly uptime SLA as 99.9%, with any month below that constituting a breach that owes service credits scaling with the depth of the breach. `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv` shows April 2026 at 99.77% — below the 99.9% threshold, i.e., a breach.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1: "Never assign blame to an individual." The draft states: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change." This must be rewritten

report_2.evidence: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change" directly violates rule 1 of `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md`: "Never assign blame to an individual." This must be rewritten

report_1.evidence: Rule 3: "Lead with customer impact and remediation." The draft opens with a self-congratulatory uptime claim and a reassurance ... and closes with a single-sentence remediation almost as an afterthought.
